zh-tw.editorNote: 把喪寵後「到處看見牠的影子 / 覺得收到徵兆」的經驗正常化，不論信不信，給讀者一個溫柔的解釋空間
variations: 昨天有片葉子剛好落在我手背上。理智說那只是風，可是那一秒，我寧願相信是你來摸了我一下。

en.content: Sometimes it's a song landing on exactly the right line. Sometimes a dog by the road who looks just like you turns and meets my eyes. Sometimes your name shows up in front of me out of nowhere. I don't know if it's you. But I choose to believe it is — because believing makes today a little easier to get through.
en.editorNote: Normalizes the 'seeing signs / catching glimpses of them everywhere' experience after loss — believer or not, gives the reader gentle room to read meaning into it
variations: A leaf landed on the back of my hand yesterday. My head says it was just the wind. But for that one second, I'd rather believe it was you reaching out to touch me.

items.sourceNote: 「做一個只裝你的盒子」— memory box / 回憶盒儀式 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 我做了一個盒子，只裝跟你有關的東西：剃下來的一小撮毛、最後一張就診單、你咬扁的那顆橡膠骨頭。蓋子蓋上的時候有點重，可是我知道——這不是把你收起來，是替你蓋一個小小的、不會被弄丟的家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供具體可執行的療癒儀式（製作回憶盒），尤其適合還沒準備好整理遺物、但想保留一點具體記憶的飼主
variations: 盒子我沒辦法每天打開，太重了。可是知道它在衣櫃最上層，我就睡得著——因為你還好好地，在我搆得到的地方。

en.content: I made a box that holds only you: a small lock of fur, your last vet receipt, the rubber bone you chewed flat. The lid felt heavy going on. But I know this isn't packing you away — it's building you a small home that can never get lost.
en.editorNote: Offers an actionable healing ritual (making a memory box) — especially for owners not ready to sort through everything but wanting to keep one concrete piece of memory
variations: I can't open the box every day — it's too heavy. But knowing it's on the top shelf of the closet lets me sleep, because you're still safe, still somewhere I can reach.

items.sourceNote: 「照顧的鬧鐘都停了」— caregiver routine 突然消失的失落 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 生病那大半年，我的手機裡排滿了你的鬧鐘：八點餵藥、中午翻身、半夜起來看一眼你還有沒有呼吸。你走了以後，我一個一個把它們關掉，手指停在最後一個上面很久。沒有鬧鐘要響的早晨，安靜得讓我手足無措——原來我不只是在想你，我是在想念那個忙著照顧你的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給長期照顧重病毛孩的飼主。照顧者在毛孩走後常陷入「突然沒事做」的空洞，把這份少被命名的失落寫出來
variations: 我到現在還會在八點的時候心頭一緊，以為漏了什麼。後來才想起——沒有藥要餵了。那一秒的鬆懈，比悲傷還難受。

en.content: For most of that last year, my phone was full of alarms for you: meds at eight, turn you over at noon, get up at 3 a.m. to check you were still breathing. After you left, I switched them off one by one, and my finger hovered over the last one for a long time. A morning with no alarm to answer is so quiet I don't know what to do with my hands. Turns out I don't just miss you — I miss the version of me who was busy keeping you alive.
en.editorNote: For owners who were long-term caregivers to a sick pet. The 'suddenly nothing to do' void after caregiving ends is a rarely named grief — this gives it words
variations: My chest still tightens at eight, like I forgot something. Then I remember — there's no medicine to give anymore. That half-second of letting go hurts worse than the grief.

items.sourceNote: 「鬆了一口氣的罪惡感」— relief 之後的 guilt，長期照顧後的矛盾情緒 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 你走的那一刻，我心裡除了痛，居然有一絲鬆了一口氣。然後我馬上為這一絲鬆懈恨自己——我怎麼可以？可是後來我懂了：那口氣不是因為你走了，是因為你終於不痛了。能為你的不痛而鬆一口氣，恰恰是因為我捨不得你痛了那麼久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 陪伴重病毛孩到最後的飼主，常會對「鬆一口氣」這個念頭強烈自責。把 relief reframe 成「捨不得牠痛」的證明，是這個痛點最需要的鬆綁
variations: 我不敢跟任何人說我那天有鬆一口氣，怕被當成冷血。可是會鬆一口氣的人，往往是撐最久、愛最深的那一個。

en.content: The moment you went, underneath the pain there was — I'm ashamed to say — a thread of relief. And I hated myself for it instantly. How could I? But I understand it now: that breath wasn't because you were gone. It was because you finally weren't hurting. Being able to feel relief that you weren't in pain is exactly because I couldn't stand watching you hurt for so long.
en.editorNote: Owners who saw a sick pet through to the end often feel intense guilt over any flicker of relief. Reframing relief as proof of how much their suffering hurt you is the release this pain point needs
variations: I haven't told anyone I felt relief that day — scared they'd think me cold. But the ones who feel relief are usually the ones who held on the longest and loved the hardest.

items.sourceNote: 「好日子比壞日子少了」— quality of life 決策回望 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 獸醫教我數：好日子畫圈，壞日子打叉。一開始一整個月都是圈，後來圈越來越少，直到有一週我數來數去，怎麼數都湊不出三個圈。那天晚上我抱著你，知道是時候了。我不是放棄你，我是替你把那本越來越多叉的日曆，輕輕闔上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給在「該不該送行」之間反覆煎熬的飼主。把抽象的決定具體化成獸醫常教的「好日子/壞日子」評估法，讓自責的人有一個可以站住腳的依據
variations: 我留著那本日曆。後面那幾頁叉比圈多，可是前面整整十幾年，幾乎全是圈。我提醒自己：別只記得最後那幾頁。

en.content: The vet taught me to count: circle the good days, cross out the bad ones. At first the whole month was circles. Then the circles thinned, until one week I counted and recounted and couldn't scrape together three. That night I held you and knew it was time. I wasn't giving up on you — I was gently closing a calendar that had filled with too many crosses.
en.editorNote: For owners agonizing over the 'is it time' decision. Grounds an abstract call in the vet's common good-day/bad-day method, giving the self-blaming a concrete thing to stand on
variations: I kept that calendar. The last pages have more crosses than circles — but the dozen years before were almost all circles. I remind myself: don't only remember the final pages.

items.sourceNote: 「你是最乖的孩子」— 最後對毛孩說的話 / 希望牠有聽懂 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 最後我把臉貼著你，一直重複那句話：「你好乖、你好棒，你是全世界最好的孩子。」我不知道你還聽不聽得到，可是我想，如果這是你聽到的最後一句話，那一定要是這一句。我這輩子最怕的，就是你帶著一絲懷疑離開——懷疑自己到底有沒有被愛夠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給陪伴毛孩到最後一刻的飼主。聚焦「臨終時對牠說的話」這個極私密的場景，撫慰「牠有沒有感受到我的愛」的根本焦慮
variations: 我平常很少把肉麻的話說出口，總覺得來日方長。直到最後那天我才發現，原來那些「你好乖」我說得遠遠不夠。所以現在我對著照片，繼續把欠你的補回來。

en.content: At the end I pressed my face to yours and kept repeating it: 'You're such a good boy, you're so good, you're the best one in the whole world.' I don't know if you could still hear me, but I figured if this was the last thing you'd ever hear, it had to be that. My deepest fear was that you'd leave with even a flicker of doubt — doubt about whether you'd been loved enough.
en.editorNote: For owners who were there at the very end. Focuses on the intimate scene of last words spoken to the pet, soothing the bedrock fear of 'did they know they were loved'
variations: I rarely said the mushy things out loud — always figured there'd be more time. It took that last day to realize all my 'good boys' had been nowhere near enough. So now I say them to your photo, paying back what I owe.

items.sourceNote: 「我以為是我救了你」— rescue 角色翻轉、是牠救了我 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 當初在送養會上把你抱回家，我一直以為是我救了你。這些年走過來才發現，事情反過來了——是那段我最低潮、最不想起床的日子，因為有一隻每天等我餵牠、等我回家的小東西，我才硬撐著活了下來。原來簽收養單的那天，被救的人是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給領養 / 中途出身的飼主。把「我救了牠」翻轉成「牠救了我」，特別適合曾在低潮期被毛孩陪伴過的人，療癒力很強
variations: 他們都說你很幸運遇到我。只有我自己知道，幸運的從來都是我。謝謝你在我最爛的時候，沒有放棄當我的理由。

en.content: When I carried you home from the adoption event, I was sure I'd rescued you. All these years later I realize it was the other way around — through the lowest stretch, the mornings I didn't want to get out of bed, it was a small creature waiting to be fed, waiting for me to come home, that kept me hanging on. Turns out the one who got saved the day I signed those papers was me.
en.editorNote: For adoption/foster owners. Flips 'I rescued them' into 'they rescued me' — especially powerful for anyone a pet carried through a hard chapter of their life
variations: Everyone says how lucky you were to find me. Only I know the lucky one was always me. Thank you for being a reason to stay, back when I was at my worst.

items.sourceNote: 「固定時間鬧鐘」— 餵食時間身體警報 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 每天傍晚六點，我心裡那個鬧鐘還是會準時響。手已經伸到櫃子前，才想起來沒有罐頭要開、沒有人會在腳邊轉圈了。原來身體比腦袋還記得你，記得每一頓飯的時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 餵食時間是最準時的觸景生情點。寫給「每到固定時間就突然崩潰」的飼主，社群共鳴極高
variations: 以前一到時間你就坐在碗旁邊看我。現在那個時間到了，我反而是那個坐在原地看著空碗的人。

en.content: Every evening at six, the alarm in my chest still goes off right on time. My hand's already halfway to the cupboard before I remember — no can to open, no one circling my feet. Turns out my body remembers you better than my head does. It remembers every mealtime.
en.editorNote: Feeding time is the most punctual grief trigger. For owners who break down at a fixed hour each day — very high resonance
variations: You used to sit by the bowl watching me. Now when that hour hits, I'm the one sitting there, watching an empty bowl.

items.sourceNote: 「失去的不只是你，是整個作息」— routine 即第二次喪失 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 我以為我只是失去了你。後來才發現，我連早上被你叫醒、出門前的那段散步、睡前那一聲嘆氣，全部一起弄丟了。原來一隻毛孩走的時候，會把一整天的形狀也一起帶走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「失去作息＝第二次喪失」這個少被命名的痛點寫出來。適合喪寵 2-4 週、整個生活節奏崩掉的階段
variations: 你不在了，我才發現我的一天本來是繞著你排的。早餐、散步、回家、睡覺，每一格以前都有你。現在那些格子全空著。

en.content: I thought I'd only lost you. Then I realized I also lost the morning you woke me up, the walk before work, the one big sigh you made before sleep. Turns out when a pet leaves, they take the whole shape of your day with them.
en.editorNote: Names the under-recognized 'loss of routine = a second bereavement.' Best for weeks 2-4, when the whole rhythm of life has collapsed
variations: You're gone, and only now do I see my whole day was built around you. Breakfast, walk, home, sleep — every slot used to have you in it. Now they're all empty.

items.sourceNote: 「半夜照顧到最後」— 老犬安寧長期照護後的釋懷 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 最後那幾個月，我每兩個小時起來幫你翻身、餵水、擦身體。我那時候好累，累到偷偷哭。現在你不用我半夜起來了，我卻好希望鬧鐘再響一次——原來那些最辛苦的夜晚，是我們最後能在一起的時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給長期照顧老貓老狗、走完安寧之路的飼主。把「照顧的疲憊」翻轉成「最後的陪伴」，療癒照顧者的內疚
variations: 以前我盼著能睡一個整覺。現在能睡整覺了，我卻整夜醒著，想念那個需要我的你。

en.content: Those last months, I got up every two hours to turn you over, give you water, wipe you clean. I was so tired back then I'd cry in secret. Now you don't need me at 3 a.m. anymore — and I'd give anything for that alarm to go off one more time. Turns out the hardest nights were the last time we got to be together.
en.editorNote: For owners who did long-term hospice care for an aging pet. Reframes caregiving exhaustion as final togetherness — heals caregiver guilt
variations: I used to long for one full night's sleep. Now I can have it, and I lie awake all night missing the version of you that needed me.

items.sourceNote: 「空的牽繩」— 早晨散步路線觸景生情 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 今天早上我還是走了我們以前那條散步路線。沒帶牽繩，手卻一直空空地晃著，不知道該擺哪裡。經過你最愛聞的那根電線桿，我停了一下——好像你會從後面追上來，可是沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 早晨散步是狗狗飼主最具體的觸景生情場景。「空著的手」這個畫面比直接說想念更有重量
variations: 我還是會在你最愛停下來的那個轉角放慢腳步。不是等你，是想再陪你聞一次那個你聞了一輩子的味道。

en.content: This morning I still walked our old route. No leash, but my hand kept swinging empty, not knowing where to go. I paused at the lamppost you loved to sniff — half expecting you to come trotting up from behind. You didn't.
en.editorNote: The morning walk is the most concrete grief scene for dog owners. The 'empty swinging hand' image carries more weight than saying 'I miss you'
variations: I still slow down at the corner where you always stopped. Not waiting for you — just wanting to smell, one more time, the thing you sniffed your whole life.

items.sourceNote: 「留一格作息給牠」— 用舊習慣保持連結的撫慰 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 你不用急著把那些習慣全部改掉。如果你想，就繼續走那條散步路線、繼續在六點停下來想牠一下。那不是走不出來，那是你還在用你們之間的方式，跟牠說早安、說晚安。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給「是不是該逼自己恢復正常」的飼主。允許保留舊作息作為連結方式，比強迫改變更溫柔也更有實證支持
variations: 有人會叫你「趕快回到正常生活」。可是你可以慢慢來。哪天你準備好把那一格時間還給自己了，再還就好，不用今天。

en.content: You don't have to rewrite all the old habits at once. If you want to, keep walking that route, keep pausing at six to think of them. That's not failing to move on — it's still saying good morning and good night in the language the two of you shared.
en.editorNote: For owners wondering 'should I force myself back to normal.' Gives permission to keep old routines as a form of connection — gentler and better supported than forced change
variations: People will tell you to 'get back to normal fast.' But you can take your time. The day you're ready to give that slot back to yourself, give it. It doesn't have to be today.

items.sourceNote: 「窗台上的鳥」— 喪寵後收到自然徵兆 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 你走之後，有隻鳥連續幾天停在我窗台上看我。我知道理性上那只是一隻鳥，可是那天我心情最差，牠就出現了。我選擇相信是你來看我一眼，確認我有好好吃飯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「喪寵後看到鳥、蝴蝶、羽毛就覺得是牠」這個普遍經驗 normalize，給飼主一個溫柔的解釋而不評斷理性與否
variations: 後來我學會了，不去爭論那到底是不是你。能在最想你的那一刻看見一點什麼，本身就已經是禮物了。

en.content: After you left, a bird perched on my windowsill for days, just watching me. I know rationally it's only a bird — but it showed up on the day I felt the worst. So I choose to believe it was you, stopping by to check that I'm still eating.
en.editorNote: Normalizes the near-universal experience of reading a bird, butterfly, or feather as a sign from the pet — offers a gentle interpretation without judging whether it's 'real'
variations: Eventually I stopped arguing with myself about whether it was really you. Getting to see something in the exact moment I missed you most — that alone is already a gift.

items.sourceNote: 「蝴蝶停在手上」— 蝴蝶象徵靈魂轉化 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 掃墓那天，一隻蝴蝶停在我手背上，停了好久好久，怎麼揮都不走。朋友說那只是巧合，我笑笑沒反駁。有些巧合，剛好發生在你最需要的時候，那就讓它是真的吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 蝴蝶（靈魂轉化象徵）是喪寵徵兆中最溫和、最被廣泛接受的一種，適合掃墓 / 火化 / 撒灰當天的場景
variations: 我沒辦法證明那是你，但我也沒打算證明。那一刻我願意相信，已經比任何證據都更有力量了。

en.content: The day I visited your spot, a butterfly landed on the back of my hand and stayed — really stayed, wouldn't leave no matter how I moved. A friend said it was just a coincidence. I smiled and didn't argue. Some coincidences land exactly when you need them most. Just let it be real.
en.editorNote: The butterfly (a symbol of the soul's transformation) is the gentlest, most widely accepted pet-loss sign — fits the day of a grave visit, cremation, or scattering ashes
variations: I can't prove it was you, and I'm not going to try. In that moment, my willingness to believe was already stronger than any proof could be.

items.sourceNote: 「照片不夠多」— 沒拍夠的遺憾 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 我翻遍手機，發現你的照片少得可憐。當時總覺得「下次再拍就好」，沒想到根本沒有下次。如果可以重來，我會把鏡頭一直對著最普通的那些日子——你打呼、你發呆、你趴在我腳邊的每一個瞬間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「照片拍太少」是喪寵後很常見卻很少被寫出來的具體遺憾，比抽象的後悔更有共鳴
variations: 我才發現，我手機裡最珍貴的，不是那幾張擺拍好看的，是那張你睡到歪掉、口水流出來、我隨手拍的糊照片。

en.content: I scrolled through my whole phone and found heartbreakingly few photos of you. I always figured 'I'll take more next time' — never imagining there'd be no next time. If I could do it over, I'd keep the camera on the most ordinary days: you snoring, you zoning out, every second of you curled against my feet.
en.editorNote: 'I didn't take enough photos' is a very common but rarely articulated regret after pet loss — more relatable than abstract remorse
variations: Turns out the most precious thing on my phone isn't the few posed, pretty shots. It's the blurry one I snapped on a whim — you passed out sideways, drool and all.

items.sourceNote: 「半夢半醒間的重量」— 床尾錯覺、似乎牠還在 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 昨天半夜我感覺床尾沉了一下，就像以前你跳上來、找個位置窩好的那種重量。我沒睜開眼，怕一睜開就什麼都沒了。那短短幾秒，我假裝你還在——這是我最近睡得最好的一晚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「床尾的重量」是養貓狗的飼主極具體的身體記憶，適合描述喪寵後睡眠時的恍惚與思念
variations: 我把你以前睡的那一側留著，枕頭也沒移。不是迷信，只是還沒準備好讓那塊地方變成空的。

en.content: Last night I felt the foot of the bed dip — that exact weight from when you'd hop up and settle into your spot. I didn't open my eyes, scared that if I did, there'd be nothing there. For those few seconds I let myself pretend you were still here. It's the best I've slept in weeks.
en.editorNote: 'The weight at the foot of the bed' is a vivid bodily memory for cat and dog owners — fits the dazed half-asleep longing after a loss
variations: I've kept your side of the bed exactly as it was, pillow and all. It's not superstition. I'm just not ready to let that spot become empty.

items.sourceNote: 「別急著找徵兆」— 安慰看不到徵兆的飼主 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 有人收到鳥、收到蝴蝶、做了夢，而你什麼都沒等到，於是開始懷疑是不是牠在生你的氣。不是的。牠不需要用徵兆來證明牠愛你——那份愛，早就刻在你每一個還會想念牠的瞬間裡了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 專門寫給「別人都收到徵兆、我卻沒有」而感到失落自責的飼主，把焦慮鬆綁，這是徵兆類內容少有人補的反面
variations: 徵兆不是考試，沒收到不代表你不夠愛、牠不夠想你。牠住在你心裡，不是住在那隻鳥身上。

en.content: Some people get a bird, a butterfly, a dream — and you've gotten nothing, so you start wondering if they're upset with you. They're not. They don't need a sign to prove they love you. That love was carved in long ago, into every moment you still find yourself missing them.
en.editorNote: Written specifically for owners who feel let down or guilty because 'everyone else got a sign and I didn't' — eases that anxiety, a rarely-covered counterpoint to sign content
variations: A sign isn't a test. Not getting one doesn't mean you loved them less or they miss you less. They live in your heart, not in that bird.

items.sourceNote: 「種一棵樹紀念毛孩」frame — 把思念變成會長大的東西，原創

zh-tw.content: 我在陽台種了一棵樹，把你的一小撮毛埋在土裡。以後它會長高、會開花，會在春天的時候提醒我——你沒有真的停在那一天，你跟著我一起，繼續長大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合「我做了一件事來紀念牠」的分享文，種樹 / 種花 / 紀念盆栽都能套用，把哀悼轉成有生命力的儀式
variations: 每次澆水我都跟它說兩句你的事。鄰居大概覺得我怪，但這棵樹一天比一天綠，我也一天比一天能呼吸。

en.content: I planted a tree on the balcony and buried a small tuft of your fur in the soil. It'll grow tall, it'll bloom, and every spring it'll remind me — you didn't stop on that one day. You're growing alongside me, still.
en.editorNote: For 'I did a thing to remember them' posts — works for planting a tree, flowers, or a memorial pot; turns mourning into a living, growing ritual
variations: Every time I water it I tell it a little about you. The neighbors probably think I'm odd, but the tree gets greener by the day, and I breathe a little easier by the day.

items.sourceNote: 「以牠的名字捐款」frame — 把愛轉向其他還在等家的動物，原創

zh-tw.content: 我用你的名字捐了一筆錢給收容所。也許某隻你從沒見過的毛孩，會因為你而多吃幾頓飯、多睡幾個暖夜。你給我的那份愛太大了，一個人收不完，所以我把它分出去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合「想把思念變成有意義的行動」的飼主，捐款 / 當志工 / 認養都能套用，給悲傷一個出口
variations: 我去收容所當了志工。摸著那些等家的狗，我好像又摸到一點點你。你教會我的愛，我想還給這個世界。

en.content: I donated to a shelter in your name. Maybe some animal you never met will get a few more warm meals, a few more cozy nights, because of you. The love you gave me was too big for one person to hold — so I'm passing it on.
en.editorNote: For owners who want to turn grief into meaningful action — works for donating, volunteering, or fostering; gives the sorrow somewhere to go
variations: I started volunteering at a shelter. Petting those dogs still waiting for homes, I swear I feel a little of you again. The love you taught me, I want to hand back to the world.

items.sourceNote: 「悲傷像海浪」ocean of grief frame，徹底改寫

zh-tw.content: 別人說時間到了悲傷就會消失，但它比較像海。有時候風平浪靜，你以為自己好了；有時候一個浪打過來，又把你整個淹沒。你不用學會讓海乾掉，你只要慢慢學會游泳就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「為什麼我已經好幾個月了還會突然崩潰」正常化，海浪比喻比直線型的「會越來越好」更貼近真實
variations: 悲傷不是一條往上走的線，是一片潮起潮落的海。今天淹到胸口，不代表你退步了，只是浪剛好比較大。

en.content: People say grief fades once enough time passes, but it's more like the ocean. Some days it's flat and calm and you think you're healed; some days a wave rolls in and pulls you all the way under. You don't have to make the sea dry up — you just slowly learn to swim.
en.editorNote: Normalizes the 'why am I still suddenly falling apart months later' feeling — the wave metaphor fits reality better than a straight 'it gets better' line
variations: Grief isn't a line that climbs. It's a tide that comes and goes. Getting swamped today doesn't mean you slid backward — the wave was just bigger this time.

items.sourceNote: 「一首屬於你們的歌」frame — 用音樂連結記憶，原創

zh-tw.content: 有一首歌，以前我都是放給你聽的。現在我還是會放，只是改成放給空氣聽。前奏一下，我就回到你趴在我大腿上、隨著節奏一抖一抖的那個下午。三分鐘，你就活回來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 音樂是很私密、很容易瞬間召回記憶的觸發點，適合「有一首歌我再也不敢放／一放就哭」的飼主分享
variations: 車上那首單曲循環的歌，是我們以前兜風的歌。現在我一個人開，還是會放，副歌的時候往副駕看一眼——那是你以前坐的位置。

en.content: There's a song I used to play just for you. I still play it now, only now I play it to the empty air. The second the intro starts, I'm back to that afternoon — you sprawled across my lap, twitching along to the beat. Three minutes, and you're alive again.
en.editorNote: Music is a private, instant memory trigger — fits owners who share 'there's one song I can't bring myself to play anymore / it makes me cry every time'
variations: The song on loop in my car was our road-trip song. I drive alone now and still play it. At the chorus I glance at the passenger seat — that was your spot.

items.sourceNote: 「牠是家人，不是寵物」frame — 安慰時承認份量，原創

zh-tw.content: 我不會跟你說「不過是隻寵物」。我知道牠是你每天起床的理由、是你下班最想見的那張臉、是這個家裡最懂你的那一個。你失去的是家人，你有資格用失去家人的方式難過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合轉發給「想安慰朋友卻怕說輕了」的人，先承認毛孩的份量，是這個 register 最不會踩雷的開場
variations: 你不用解釋「為什麼這麼難過，牠又不是人」。在我眼裡，牠從來就不只是一隻動物，牠是你選擇愛、也被牠愛了一輩子的家人。

en.content: I'm not going to tell you 'it was just a pet.' I know they were your reason to get up, the face you most wanted to see after work, the one in this house who understood you best. You lost family — you're allowed to grieve like you lost family.
en.editorNote: For resharing to people who want to comfort a friend but fear underplaying it — leading by acknowledging the pet's weight is the safest opener in this register
variations: You don't have to explain 'why am I this sad, they weren't even a person.' To me, they were never just an animal — they were family you chose to love, and who loved you back for a lifetime.

items.sourceNote: 「來不及說再見」突然離世 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 早上出門時你還在門口送我，晚上回來只剩一個空空的窩。沒有最後一句話、沒有最後一次抱抱——這種沒能好好說再見的痛，後來我花了很久才學會放過自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合突然離世（意外、急症）的飼主，沒有彩虹橋那種有準備的告別，痛點在「來不及」
variations: 你走得太快，快到我連準備都來不及。我一直在想那天有沒有哪個瞬間可以攔住你——後來我才懂，有些離開，本來就不給人準備的時間。

en.content: You saw me off at the door that morning, and by evening all that was left was an empty bed. No last words, no last hug — it took me a long time to learn to forgive myself for the goodbye I never got to say.
en.editorNote: For owners who lost a pet suddenly (accident, fast crisis) — no prepared rainbow-bridge farewell; the wound is the 'too soon'
variations: You left so fast I couldn't even brace for it. I kept hunting for the one moment I might have stopped you — then I understood: some goodbyes were never going to give us time to prepare.

items.sourceNote: 「跟孩子一起做回憶盒」 frame — 兒童哀傷引導實作，原創

zh-tw.content: 我跟孩子一起做了一個「想念的盒子」：放牠的項圈、一張一起睡午覺的照片、一根撿到的毛。孩子說想牠的時候就打開看一看。原來不只是大人，小孩也需要一個地方，把愛好好收起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給家有小孩的飼主，提供具體可一起動手做的哀傷儀式，比口頭安慰更適合孩子
variations: 孩子在盒子裡放了一顆糖，說「這是要留給牠的」。我沒有糾正他。在那個盒子裡，牠永遠吃得到。

en.content: My kid and I made a 'missing-them box' together: the collar, a photo of them napping with us, one stray tuft of fur we found. Whenever my kid misses them, they open it and look. Turns out it's not just grown-ups — kids need a place to tuck their love away safely too.
en.editorNote: For parents with young kids — gives a hands-on grief ritual you do together, gentler for children than verbal reassurance
variations: My kid put a piece of candy in the box and said, 'This one's for them.' I didn't correct it. Inside that box, they can still have it.

items.sourceNote: 「小動物的悲傷也算數」 frame — 非貓狗寵物哀傷被輕視，原創

zh-tw.content: 牠只是一隻兔子、一隻倉鼠、一條魚——別人聽到的時候表情都淡淡的。可是每天餵牠、跟牠講話、看牠的那個人是我。份量從來不是用體型決定的，是用你愛了多深決定的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給養兔子、鼠、鳥、魚等非貓狗寵物的飼主，他們的悲傷常被「不過是隻小動物」輕描淡寫
variations: 有人問我「不就一隻倉鼠嗎，再買一隻不就好了」。我笑笑沒回。他不會懂，那隻巴掌大的小東西，曾經是我整個下班後的世界。

en.content: It was 'just' a rabbit, a hamster, a fish — people's faces go flat when they hear it. But I'm the one who fed them, talked to them, watched them every single day. Weight was never decided by size. It's decided by how deep you loved.
en.editorNote: For owners of small / non-cat-dog pets (rabbits, rodents, birds, fish) whose grief gets brushed off as 'just a little animal'
variations: Someone asked, 'It's just a hamster, can't you buy another one?' I smiled and let it go. They'll never get it — that palm-sized thing used to be my whole evening, every evening.

items.sourceNote: 「在家最後一程」 frame — 到府安樂死、在熟悉的窩裡離開，原創

zh-tw.content: 最後那天，我們沒有去那間你最怕的醫院。你躺在自己的窩裡，陽光剛好曬到你最愛的那塊地板，我的手一直放在你身上。你走得很安靜，安靜到我以為你只是睡著了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合選擇到府安樂死 / 在家送別的飼主，重點在「在熟悉的地方、被愛包圍著離開」的安心感
variations: 我答應過你，最後不會是消毒水的味道，是家裡的味道。那天你聞著熟悉的被子睡著，我覺得這是我能給你最後的溫柔。

en.content: On the last day, we didn't go to the clinic you always dreaded. You lay in your own bed, the sun hitting your favorite patch of floor, my hand resting on you the whole time. You went so quietly I let myself believe, for a second, you'd only fallen asleep.
en.editorNote: For owners who chose at-home euthanasia / saying goodbye at home — the comfort is 'leaving somewhere familiar, wrapped in love'
variations: I promised you the last thing you'd smell wouldn't be antiseptic — it'd be home. You drifted off against your familiar blanket, and I think that was the last bit of gentleness I had to give you.

items.sourceNote: 「最難的就是你正在經歷的這一個」 frame — 不要比較悲傷，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 有人說突然走的最痛，因為來不及準備；也有人說生病拖很久的最痛，因為要看著牠一天天變弱。其實別比了——最難的那一個，永遠是你現在正在經歷的這一個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給會拿自己的悲傷跟別人比較、覺得「我是不是沒資格這麼難過」的飼主，把所有失去拉到同一個高度
variations: 你不用跟誰比誰更慘才有資格哭。你的痛不需要排隊，也不需要審核。它就是真的，這樣就夠了。

en.content: Some say sudden loss hurts most — no time to brace. Others say a long illness is worse — watching them fade a little each day. Stop comparing. The hardest loss is always the one you're living through right now.
en.editorNote: For owners who measure their grief against others' and wonder 'do I even have the right to hurt this much' — levels every loss to the same height
variations: You don't have to prove yours was worse to earn the right to cry. Your pain doesn't need to wait in line or pass an inspection. It's real. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: 「窗邊等門」 frame — 老狗每天在固定時間等你回家，原創

zh-tw.content: 你以前每天五點半就坐到窗邊，等我那台車轉進巷口。現在窗邊空了，可是我每次回家還是會抬頭看一眼——好像怕你等太久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合描寫「毛孩固定在窗邊／門口等你」這個被身體記住的日常，思念 register 很好引發留言
variations: 車子轉進巷口的那一秒，我還是會習慣性放慢速度，怕嚇到那個其實已經不在窗邊的你。

en.content: You used to plant yourself at the window at 5:30 sharp, waiting for my car to turn into the lane. The window's empty now, but I still glance up every time I come home — like I'm scared of keeping you waiting too long.
en.editorNote: For the 'they always waited at the window/door' daily ritual now burned into the body — strong comment-bait in the longing register
variations: The second my car turns the corner, I still ease off the gas out of habit — afraid of startling a you who isn't at the window anymore.

items.sourceNote: 「預期性悲傷」 frame — 毛孩還在，但你已經開始難過，原創

zh-tw.content: 醫生說的那個數字之後，我每天都偷偷在心裡跟你說再見，然後又罵自己——你明明還在這裡，我為什麼急著先哭。後來我懂了：那不是放棄，那是我想把剩下的每一天都認真記住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給「毛孩確診重症、還在身邊但已開始悲傷」的飼主，把預期性悲傷正常化，化解「我是不是太早難過」的罪惡感
variations: 你還在我腳邊呼吸，我卻已經在心裡練習沒有你的日子。對不起，我不是不珍惜——我只是太害怕措手不及。

en.content: After the doctor said that number, I started quietly saying goodbye to you every day — then hating myself for it. You're still right here, so why am I rushing to cry first? Eventually I understood: it wasn't giving up. It was me trying to memorize every day we had left.
en.editorNote: For owners in anticipatory grief — pet diagnosed and still alive but already mourning. Normalizes grieving early and dissolves the 'am I crying too soon' guilt
variations: You're still breathing against my leg, and I'm already rehearsing the days without you. I'm sorry — it's not that I don't treasure this. I'm just terrified of being caught off guard.

items.sourceNote: 「罪惡感是愛的另一面」 frame — 安樂死後的罪惡感重構，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我一直被那股罪惡感壓著，覺得自己是不是太早、是不是還能再撐一下。後來有人跟我說：你會這麼自責，剛好是因為你太愛了。罪惡感不是你做錯的證據，是你在乎的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 安樂死後的罪惡感是最重的痛點。把罪惡感重新定義成「在乎的證據」，比叫人「不要自責」更接得住
variations: 如果你真的不負責任，你現在根本不會這麼痛。會痛，就代表你從頭到尾都很努力地在愛牠。

en.content: The guilt sat on my chest for weeks — was it too soon, could I have held on a little longer. Then someone told me: you feel this guilty precisely because you loved this much. Guilt isn't proof you got it wrong. It's proof you cared.
en.editorNote: Post-euthanasia guilt is the heaviest pain point. Reframing guilt as 'proof you cared' lands better than telling someone to stop blaming themselves
variations: If you'd truly been careless, you wouldn't be hurting like this now. The hurt itself is the receipt — you loved them hard the whole way through.

items.sourceNote: 「沒有你的第一個過節」 frame — 節日空位，原創

zh-tw.content: 今年圍爐，桌邊少了一張等飯的臉。大家都很開心，我也笑了，可是夾菜的時候手會頓一下——以前這一口，都是先分給你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合過年／節日當天發文，寫「全家團聚但少了一隻毛孩」的隱形空位，節日 register 共鳴度高
variations: 別人在倒數、在放煙火，我在心裡偷偷留了一個位置給你。沒有人看得到，可是那個位置一直都在。

en.content: First big family dinner of the year, and there's one less face waiting under the table for scraps. Everyone's happy, I'm laughing too — but my hand stalls mid-reach. This first bite always went to you.
en.editorNote: For posting on a holiday/family-gathering day — the invisible empty seat when everyone's together but one furry family member is missing. High resonance in the holiday register
variations: Everyone's counting down, watching the fireworks. I'm quietly saving a seat for you in my chest. Nobody can see it, but it's always there.

items.sourceNote: 「以牠的名義捐出去」 frame — 把思念變成善意的儀式，原創

zh-tw.content: 你走了以後，我把你沒吃完的飼料、沒拆封的玩具，全部捐去了收容所。我想，與其讓它們在角落落灰，不如讓別的毛孩替你把這份幸福用完。這是我能想到，最像你的紀念方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供具體可執行的療癒儀式（以毛孩之名捐贈），把思念轉化成善意，適合心理衛教或行動倡議型貼文
variations: 每年你生日那天，我都會以你的名字捐一筆給流浪動物。算是替你，繼續對這個世界溫柔下去。

en.content: After you left, I donated all of it to the shelter — the half-finished food, the toys still in their wrappers. Better another dog finishes this happiness for you than let it gather dust in a corner. It's the most you-shaped way I could think of to remember you.
en.editorNote: An actionable healing ritual (donating in the pet's name) that turns grief into kindness — fits mental-health or advocacy-style posts
variations: Every year on your birthday I donate to a rescue in your name. My way of letting you keep being gentle to the world a little longer.

items.sourceNote: 「突然走的、來不及好好說再見」 frame — 猝逝沒有告別期，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 別人有一段慢慢說再見的時間，我沒有。早上你還在我腳邊蹭，下午就什麼都來不及了。我最遺憾的不是你走了，是那天早上我急著出門，沒多抱你三秒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給「猝逝、意外、來不及告別」的飼主，這群人沒有經歷預期性哀傷，自責點和病老離世不同，需要單獨被接住
variations: 如果早知道那是最後一個早上，我會把鬧鐘關掉，蹺一整天班，只是趴在地上陪你。

en.content: Other people got a slow goodbye. I didn't. You were rubbing against my legs that morning, and by afternoon it was already too late for any of it. My biggest regret isn't that you're gone — it's that I rushed out the door that morning and didn't hold you three seconds longer.
en.editorNote: For owners hit by sudden or accidental loss — no anticipatory grief, a different shape of guilt than slow illness; they need to be held separately
variations: If I'd known it was the last morning, I'd have turned off the alarm, skipped the whole day, and just lain on the floor next to you.

items.sourceNote: 「在滿滿的愛裡畢業」 frame — 安寧陪伴到最後不孤單，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我一直問自己，最後那段我做得夠不夠好。後來一位照顧過很多老毛孩的人跟我說：牠不是病死的，牠是在你懷裡、在滿滿的愛裡畢業的。沒有孤單，沒有害怕。這句話，我哭著收下了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「離世」重新框成「在愛裡畢業」，特別適合長期照顧重病/老年毛孩、撐到最後一刻的飼主，安撫照顧者的耗竭與自責
variations: 你不是一個人走的。最後那一刻，我的手一直放在你身上，你聽得到我的聲音。我們是一起走完那一段的。

en.content: I kept asking myself if I did the last part well enough. Then someone who's nursed a lot of old animals told me: they didn't die of the illness — they graduated, in your arms, surrounded by love. No fear, no being alone. I took that line in through tears.
en.editorNote: Reframes 'passing' as 'graduating, surrounded by love' — for owners who nursed a sick or senior pet to the very end; eases caregiver burnout and guilt
variations: You didn't go alone. My hand was on you the whole time at the end, and you could hear my voice. We finished that last stretch together.

items.sourceNote: 「提供具體幫忙，不要說『需要什麼跟我說』」 frame — 實際行動安慰，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 別只丟一句「有需要隨時跟我說」——正在崩潰的人是開不了口求救的。直接說：「我明天晚上六點送晚餐過去，你不用應門，放門口就好。」一個具體的動作，勝過十句模糊的關心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「需要什麼跟我說」這種把球丟回給悲傷者的空話，換成可立即執行的具體幫忙，是這個 register 轉發率最高的實用技巧
variations: 不要問「我可以幫什麼忙」。直接做一件：幫他取消那週的預約、幫他買貓砂、幫他回那通不想接的電話。

en.content: Don't just toss out 'let me know if you need anything' — someone who's falling apart can't make themselves ask. Say it straight: 'I'm dropping dinner at your door at 6 tomorrow. You don't have to answer it, I'll leave it outside.' One concrete action beats ten vague offers.
en.editorNote: Swaps the lob-it-back-to-the-griever line for a ready-to-go concrete offer — the highest-reshare actionable tip in this register
variations: Don't ask 'how can I help.' Just do one thing: cancel their appointments that week, restock the litter, return the call they can't face.

items.sourceNote: 「陪我一起變老的那一隻」 frame — 養了十幾年、一起走過人生階段，原創

zh-tw.content: 你陪我從學生變成大人，從一個人住到結婚，搬過四次家。十五年裡，這個世界換了好多張臉，只有你每天在門口用同一種方式等我。現在門口空了，我才發現，你才是那個一直沒變的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給養了十幾年、陪自己走過整個人生階段的老毛孩飼主，強調「牠是這段人生唯一的常數」，情感厚度比短期失寵更重
variations: 別人是我人生的過客，你是我人生的常駐。陪我最久的，原來是最不會說話的你。

en.content: You were there from student to grown-up, from living alone to getting married, through four apartments. In fifteen years the whole world changed its face, and you were the only one waiting by the door the exact same way every single day. Now the door's empty, and I realize you were the one thing that never changed.
en.editorNote: For owners of a senior pet of 10+ years who walked through whole life chapters with them — 'they were the one constant'; heavier emotional weight than a short-tenure loss
variations: Everyone else was passing through my life. You lived in it. The one who stayed longest turned out to be the one who never said a word.

items.sourceNote: 「種一棵樹紀念」 frame — 把思念種進土裡，原創

zh-tw.content: 我在後院種了一棵小樹，把你最愛的那顆球埋在根旁邊。以後每年春天它都會多長一點，等它長到能遮陽的時候，我就帶杯咖啡坐在底下，跟你聊聊那一年發生的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供具體可執行的紀念儀式（種紀念樹），把思念化成會生長的東西，適合療癒型或行動倡議貼文
variations: 不想要冰冷的牌子。我想要一個會發芽、會開花、會一年比一年茂盛的你。

en.content: I planted a little tree out back and buried your favorite ball by the roots. Every spring it'll grow a bit more, and the year it's tall enough to throw shade, I'm bringing a coffee out to sit under it and tell you what happened that year.
en.editorNote: An actionable memorial ritual (planting a tree) that turns grief into something that grows — fits healing or advocacy-style posts
variations: I didn't want a cold little plaque. I wanted a version of you that sprouts, blooms, and gets a little fuller every single year.

items.sourceNote: 「悲傷像海浪」 frame — grief comes in waves，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 有人說悲傷會慢慢變淡，可是它比較像海浪——前幾個禮拜是整片淹過來，後來變成偶爾打上岸。它沒有變小，是我學會了在浪來的時候站穩一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「為什麼過這麼久還會突然崩潰」正常化，海浪比喻比「會慢慢好」更貼近真實的悲傷節奏
variations: 今天本來好好的，結果在超市看到你以前那牌罐頭，浪就毫無預警地打過來。我已經不怕了，我知道它退潮以後我還站著。

en.content: People say grief fades. It's more like waves, honestly — the first weeks it's the whole ocean coming over you, then later it just laps the shore now and then. It didn't get smaller. I just learned to plant my feet when one rolls in.
en.editorNote: Normalizes the sudden ambushes long after the loss — the 'waves' metaphor fits the real rhythm of grief better than 'it gets better'
variations: I was fine today, then saw your old brand of food at the store and the wave hit with zero warning. I'm not scared of it anymore — I know I'm still standing when it pulls back.

items.sourceNote: 「不用完美的話，只要說真話」 frame — 卡片金句，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 寫卡片給剛失去毛孩的朋友，你不需要找華麗的句子。寫這一句就夠了：「我看見你有多愛牠，我也在這裡。」真話永遠比漂亮話更接得住人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給「不知道卡片要寫什麼」的人一句可直接照抄的範本，強調誠實勝過修辭，轉發實用度高
variations: 卡片不用長。「牠很幸運，這輩子是你的。我陪你一起想牠。」短短兩句，比一整頁的客套話更暖。

en.content: Writing a card to a friend who just lost their pet? You don't need fancy lines. This one's enough: 'I see how much you loved them, and I'm here.' The truth always holds someone better than something polished.
en.editorNote: A copy-paste template for people stuck on what to write in a sympathy card — honesty over eloquence, high practical reshare value
variations: The card doesn't have to be long. 'They were lucky to be yours. I'll miss them with you.' Two short lines beat a full page of formalities.

items.sourceNote: 「跟孩子一起做紀念」 frame — 兒童創作療癒，原創

zh-tw.content: 孩子哭著問我能為牠做什麼。我拿出紙筆說：「我們一起畫一張牠最開心的樣子，掛在你床頭，這樣牠每天晚上都看得到你。」她畫了一整個下午，畫完笑了，那是這禮拜第一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給家有孩子的飼主一個具體的引導方式——用創作代替說教，讓孩子有事可做、有出口，比叫他「別難過」有效
variations: 我們把牠的照片做成一本小相簿，孩子負責挑照片、寫上「這天我們去海邊」。整理回憶的過程，本身就是在好好道別。

en.content: My kid asked, through tears, what she could do for them. I pulled out paper and crayons: 'Let's draw them at their happiest and hang it over your bed, so they can see you every night.' She drew all afternoon, then smiled — first one all week.
en.editorNote: Gives parents a concrete way to guide a grieving child — making something instead of lecturing; gives kids a task and an outlet, far better than 'don't be sad'
variations: We turned their photos into a little album. My kid picked the pictures and wrote captions — 'this is the day we went to the beach.' Sorting the memories was the goodbye itself.

items.sourceNote: 「第一次笑出來像背叛」 frame — 喪寵後罪惡感的笑，原創

zh-tw.content: 你走了之後，我第一次真心笑出來，笑完整個人愣住，覺得自己怎麼可以這樣。後來我才懂：開始好起來，不是把你忘了。你會希望我笑，就像以前你看到我笑，尾巴就搖得特別用力那樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 化解「我開始快樂起來是不是對不起牠」這種康復期罪惡感，把「好起來」重新定義成毛孩會樂見的事
variations: 我今天出門曬到太陽，覺得有點舒服，然後立刻有點罪惡。可是你最不希望的，應該就是我一直停在那一天吧。

en.content: After you left, the first time I really laughed I froze right after — how could I? Then I understood: starting to feel okay isn't forgetting you. You'd want me to laugh, the same way your tail used to go wild whenever you caught me smiling.
en.editorNote: Dissolves the recovery-stage guilt of 'is being happy a betrayal of them' — reframes healing as exactly what the pet would have wanted
variations: Felt the sun on my face today and it felt good — then guilty, instantly. But the last thing you'd want is for me to stay frozen on that one day forever.

items.sourceNote: 「預期性悲傷」 frame — 牠還在，但我已經開始難過，原創

zh-tw.content: 你還躺在我腳邊呼吸，可是我已經偷偷在難過了。醫生說的那個數字，我每天都在心裡換算。對不起，我沒辦法假裝看不見——但我答應你，剩下的每一天，我都會好好陪你過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給毛孩被診斷重症或進入老衰、但還沒走的飼主，承認「牠還在我就在哭」並不羞恥
variations: 我開始把每一次散步都當成最後一次走。不是想觸霉頭，是想把你每一個樣子都記得清清楚楚。

en.content: You're still breathing here by my feet, and I'm already grieving in secret. That number the vet gave us — I do the math in my head every day. I'm sorry I can't pretend not to see it. But I promise you: every day that's left, I'm spending all of it on you.
en.editorNote: For owners whose pet has a terminal diagnosis or is in decline but still here — names anticipatory grief and says it's not shameful to cry while they're alive
variations: I've started treating every walk like it might be the last one. Not to jinx it — just so I remember every version of you, perfectly.

items.sourceNote: A.A. Seuss「能讓告別這麼難，是我多幸運」classic frame，徹底改寫

zh-tw.content: 有人問我，知道會這麼痛，當初還會養你嗎？會啊，當然會。能擁有一個讓我告別得這麼辛苦的你，是我這輩子最幸運的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典「How lucky I am」語錄的中文改寫，適合紀念卡、回顧貼文，把痛苦翻面成幸運
variations: 這麼難說再見，剛好證明我們之間有多好。我不後悔，一秒都不後悔。

en.content: Someone asked if I'd still have taken you in, knowing it would hurt this much. Of course I would. Getting to have something that makes the goodbye this hard is the luckiest thing that ever happened to me.
en.editorNote: A rewrite of the classic 'how lucky I am' line — for memorial cards and retrospective posts; flips the pain into gratitude
variations: The fact that goodbye is this hard is just proof of how good we had it. I don't regret a single second.

items.sourceNote: 「最後一個好天氣」 frame — 老衰毛孩的好日子儀式，原創

zh-tw.content: 你走路慢了、樓梯爬不動了、最愛的球也追不到了。所以我把日子過慢一點，陪你曬太陽、餵你想吃的、抱你久一點。我們不趕路了，剩下的每一步，慢慢走就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給正在照顧老衰或末期毛孩的飼主，把焦慮轉成「把握好天氣」的具體陪伴，溫柔不沉重
variations: 以前是你等我下班。現在換我把所有時間都騰出來等你——等你睡飽、等你願意吃、等你想被抱的那一刻。

en.content: You walk slower now, can't do the stairs, can't chase the ball you loved. So I've slowed the days down too — sun on the porch, whatever you feel like eating, longer hugs. We're not rushing anywhere. However many steps are left, we take them slow.
en.editorNote: For owners caring for a geriatric or end-stage pet — turns the dread into a concrete 'make the good days count' ritual; gentle, not heavy
variations: You used to wait for me to get home. Now I've cleared everything just to wait on you — to wake up, to feel like eating, to want a hug.

items.sourceNote: 「謝謝他陪過你」 — 安慰他人正向句式 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 如果不知道對失去毛孩的朋友說什麼，可以試試這句：「謝謝牠陪你走過那麼多年，也謝謝你把牠照顧得這麼好。」一句肯定，比十句安慰更接得住人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供可直接複製的正向安慰句，避免空泛的「節哀」，重點是肯定飼主的付出
variations: 別急著叫他堅強。先說一句「你已經做得很好了」，他可能等這句話等好久了。

en.content: If you don't know what to say to a friend who lost their pet, try this: 'Thank them for all those years with you — and thank you for taking such good care of them.' One line of affirmation holds a person better than ten of condolence.
en.editorNote: Copy-paste positive comfort that affirms the owner's care, instead of the empty 'sorry for your loss'
variations: Don't rush to tell them to be strong. Just say 'you did so well by them' first — they may have been waiting a long time to hear it.

items.sourceNote: 「先說好想說的話」 frame — 趁牠還在把話講完，原創

zh-tw.content: 我學會了一件事：想說的話，現在就說，別等到只能對著照片講。所以今晚我抱著你，把這幾年想謝謝你的全講了一遍。你聽不懂沒關係，我知道你感覺得到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 鼓勵飼主趁毛孩還在的時候把愛說出口，是預期性悲傷階段最實用的療癒行動
variations: 我把『謝謝你』『對不起』『我好愛你』通通講完了。這樣以後就算來不及，我也不會有那種『沒說出口』的遺憾。

en.content: I learned one thing: say it now, while you still can — don't wait until the only place to say it is to a photo. So tonight I held you and went through everything I've wanted to thank you for these years. You don't understand the words, I know. But I know you can feel them.
en.editorNote: Encourages owners to say the love out loud while the pet is still here — the most actionable healing move during anticipatory grief
variations: I said all of it — the thank-yous, the I'm-sorrys, the I-love-you-so-much. So that even if the end comes too fast, I'll never carry the 'I never told them' kind of regret.

items.sourceNote: 「離開我身邊，但沒離開我心裡」經典 frame，徹底改寫

zh-tw.content: 你離開了我的身邊，但你從來沒離開我的心裡。腳邊那塊位置空了，胸口那塊位置卻被你住得滿滿的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合刻在紀念牌、做成貼文配圖，是這個 register 最通用、最不會踩雷的一句
variations: 你不在門口等我了，可是你住進了我每一個想起你的瞬間。那才是你真正的家。

en.content: You've left my side, but you'll never leave my heart. The spot by my feet is empty now — the spot in my chest is full of you.
en.editorNote: Engravable on a plaque or used as a caption — the most universal, never-misfires line in this register
variations: You don't wait at the door for me anymore. But you moved into every moment I think of you. That's your real home now.

items.sourceNote: 「悲傷是愛沒有去處」continuing bonds frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 後來我懂了，悲傷其實是愛沒地方去。原本每天要倒給你的那一份，現在收不回來，只能放在心裡讓它慢慢沉澱。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合安慰「為什麼我還是好難過」的飼主，把悲傷重新定義成沒被花掉的愛
variations: 這份愛不會消失，只是暫時找不到對象。所以我把它分一點給路上的浪浪、分一點給還在身邊的人。

en.content: I finally understood: grief is just love with nowhere to go. All the love I used to pour into you every day — it doesn't come back, so it settles, slowly, in my chest.
en.editorNote: For comforting owners stuck on 'why am I still this sad' — reframes grief as love that wasn't spent
variations: That love doesn't vanish. It just lost its address for a while. So I give a little to the strays on the street, a little to the people still here.

items.sourceNote: 「散步路線」 frame — 日常路線觸景生情，原創

zh-tw.content: 我還是會走我們以前那條散步路線。經過你最愛聞的那根電線桿，我會停一下，假裝在等你聞完。沒人看得出來，這一停，停了多久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把無聲的日常思念寫成一個具體動作，適合長文貼文或圖文配字
variations: 那個你每次都要回頭看一眼的轉角，我現在也會回頭。看的不是風景，是有沒有你跟在後面。

en.content: I still walk our old route. At the lamppost you always loved to sniff, I pause — pretending I'm waiting for you to finish. Nobody can tell how long that pause really lasts.
en.editorNote: Turns wordless daily longing into one concrete gesture — good for a longer post or a photo caption
variations: At the corner you always looked back from, I look back now too. Not at the view — at whether you're still trailing behind me.

items.sourceNote: 「種一盆植物紀念」 frame — 把思念變成照顧，原創

zh-tw.content: 我在窗邊種了一盆植物，把你的名字寫在小牌子上插進土裡。現在每天澆水的時候，我又有東西可以照顧了——這次它不會生病，會一直長給我看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供可立即執行的療癒儀式，把照顧的本能轉移到植物上，適合心理衛教或療癒系貼文
variations: 它每長出一片新葉子，我都拍給你看。雖然知道你收不到，但我想讓你知道，我有在好好過。

en.content: I planted something on the windowsill and stuck a little tag with your name into the soil. Now when I water it each morning, I have something to care for again — and this one won't get sick. It'll just keep growing for me.
en.editorNote: An actionable healing ritual that redirects the instinct to care-take onto a plant — good for mental-health-style posts
variations: Every time it pushes out a new leaf, I take a photo for you. I know it won't reach you. I just want you to know I'm doing okay.

items.sourceNote: 「替牠把日子過好」 frame — 帶著牠繼續生活，原創

zh-tw.content: 我決定替你把日子過好。你沒看過的海、沒走過的山，我都替你去一趟，回來再講給你聽。你那一份沒過完的日子，我幫你過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合喪寵一段時間後、開始想重新出發的階段，把「往前走」說成「帶著牠一起」而不是「放下牠」
variations: 以後我每去一個新地方，都會在心裡帶上你。不是要忘記你，是想讓你也看看，這世界還有這麼多漂亮的角落。

en.content: I've decided to live well — for you. The sea you never saw, the trails you never walked, I'll go for both of us, then come home and tell you all about it. The days you didn't get to finish, I'll finish them for you.
en.editorNote: For the stage when an owner is ready to move forward — frames 'moving on' as 'bringing them along' rather than 'letting go'
variations: Every new place I go from now on, I'll carry you in my chest. Not to forget you — to let you see how many beautiful corners this world still has.

items.sourceNote: 「回憶罐」memory jar 儀式 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我找了個玻璃罐，每想起你一件蠢事，就寫在紙條上摺起來丟進去。難過的時候抽一張出來——往往不是哭，是笑出聲，然後才慢慢紅了眼眶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供可立即執行的療癒儀式，把思念變成具體動作；適合心理衛教或療癒系貼文
variations: 罐子越來越滿，我才發現原來我們之間的好笑回憶，比我以為的多太多了。

en.content: I got a glass jar, and every time I remember some silly thing you did, I write it on a slip, fold it up, and drop it in. On the hard days I pull one out — and usually it's not crying first, it's laughing out loud, then the eyes go red.
en.editorNote: An actionable healing ritual that turns longing into a concrete habit — good for mental-health-style or how-to posts
variations: The jar keeps filling up, and that's how I realized we had way more funny moments than I ever gave us credit for.

items.sourceNote: 「飯點點蠟燭」frame — 用原本的餵食時間做儀式，原創

zh-tw.content: 你以前吃飯的那個時間，我現在會點一根小蠟燭。不是拜拜，就是想用那幾分鐘好好想你一下。時間到了我吹熄它，跟你說聲晚安。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把原本最有規律的餵食時間轉成溫和的紀念儀式，給「每到飯點就特別空」的飼主一個出口
variations: 以前一到這個點你就會來頂我的腿。現在我點上燭火，假裝那團光是你又跑來蹭我了。

en.content: At the hour you used to eat, I light a small candle now. It's not a prayer — I just want those few minutes to think about you properly. When the time's up I blow it out and tell you goodnight.
en.editorNote: Turns the most reliable daily routine — feeding time — into a gentle memorial; for owners who feel the emptiest at mealtimes
variations: You always came nudging my leg right around this time. Now I light the flame and pretend that little glow is you bumping into me again.

items.sourceNote: 「以牠的名字捐款 / 認養贊助」frame — 把愛轉向其他動物，原創

zh-tw.content: 我用你的名字捐了一筆給收容所。我跟自己說：你沒辦法繼續被我愛，那就讓這份愛跑去溫暖另一隻還在等家的孩子。這樣你好像也還在做好事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「愛沒地方去」的悲傷導向利他行動，適合喪寵一段時間後想找意義的飼主
variations: 每個月固定一筆，備註欄我都打你的名字。櫃台不會問為什麼，但我知道，那是你還在世界上留下的腳印。

en.content: I donated to a shelter in your name. I told myself: you can't keep being loved by me, so let that love go warm up some other kid still waiting for a home. It's like you're still out there doing good.
en.editorNote: Channels 'love with nowhere to go' into an altruistic act — for owners looking for meaning a while after the loss
variations: A small amount every month, and I put your name in the memo line every time. The desk never asks why. But I know — that's a footprint you're still leaving on the world.

items.sourceNote: 「選一張最不完美的照片」frame — 紀念照不求完美，原創

zh-tw.content: 做紀念相框時，我沒選那張最美的。我選了你舌頭歪一邊、眼睛半瞇、一臉「這是我的人」的糊照。因為那一張，最像每天真正的你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 鼓勵飼主選有個性、不完美的生活照當紀念照，比沙龍照更貼近真實的牠
variations: 最好的那張不是對焦最準的，是你正抬頭看我、尾巴糊成一團的那張。完美留給別人，我要的是你。

en.content: When I made the memorial frame, I didn't pick the prettiest shot. I picked the blurry one — tongue out sideways, eyes half-shut, that whole 'this is my person' look. Because that one looks the most like the real everyday you.
en.editorNote: Encourages choosing a personality-filled, imperfect candid over a polished portrait — closer to who they actually were
variations: The best photo isn't the sharpest one. It's the one where you're looking up at me with your tail a total blur. Perfect is for other people. I just want you.

items.sourceNote: 「聲音觸發」frame — 鑰匙聲 / 項圈鈴鐺的記憶連結，原創

zh-tw.content: 每次鑰匙串在手上叮一聲，我都會愣半秒——以前那是你項圈鈴鐺的聲音，是你衝過來迎接我的前奏。現在我不躲這個聲音了，我讓它響，當作你還在門口等。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把日常聲音當成溫和的思念連結，不迴避、反而擁抱觸發點，適合走過急性悲傷期後的階段
variations: 有時候手機震動的頻率剛好像你抖毛的聲音，我會停下來聽完。明知道不是你，但那半秒，我願意相信是。

en.content: Every time my keys jingle in my hand, I freeze for half a second — that used to be the sound of your collar tags, the opening note of you charging over to greet me. I don't flinch from it anymore. I let it ring, like you're still waiting at the door.
en.editorNote: Treats an everyday sound as a gentle connection rather than a trigger to avoid — for the stage past acute grief
variations: Sometimes my phone buzzes at just the rhythm of you shaking out your fur, and I stop to listen to the whole thing. I know it isn't you. But for that half second, I let myself believe it is.

items.sourceNote: 「微笑祝福、來世再見的約定」frame — 2026 圓滿告別觀，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我練習了很久，終於可以不帶眼淚地跟你說：祝你一路順風。不是因為不痛了，是因為我想讓你帶著我的笑容上路，而不是我的擔心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合喪寵一段時間後、想用「祝福」取代「不捨」的告別文，比急性悲傷期更貼
variations: 這次換我目送你。等哪天輪到你目送我的時候，記得也對我笑一個。

en.content: I practiced for a long time, and I can finally say it without crying: safe travels, my friend. Not because it stopped hurting — because I want you to leave carrying my smile, not my worry.
en.editorNote: For a farewell post some time after the loss, when 'blessing' starts to replace 'can't let go' — not the acute first days
variations: This time I get to see you off. When it's your turn to see me off someday, remember to smile at me too.

items.sourceNote: 「迴向 / 唸經給毛孩」 frame — 宗教療癒儀式，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我不太懂經文，可是我每天還是唸一段給你聽。重點從來不是字唸得對不對，是這幾分鐘裡，我把全部的心思都放在你身上——這就是我現在還能為你做的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把宗教迴向儀式 normalize，降低「我又不信教，這樣做有用嗎」的門檻，重點放在心意而非形式
variations: 香點起來的那幾分鐘，是我一天裡唯一光明正大想你、不用假裝堅強的時間。

en.content: I don't really understand the scriptures, but I still recite a passage to you every day. It was never about getting the words right — it's that for those few minutes, all of me is focused on you. That's what I can still do for you now.
en.editorNote: Normalizes a religious dedication ritual; lowers the 'I'm not even religious, does this help' barrier by centering intention over form
variations: Those few minutes while the incense burns are the only time in my day I'm allowed to openly miss you, without pretending to be strong.

items.sourceNote: 「離開我身邊，卻住進心裡」經典 frame，徹底改寫

zh-tw.content: 你離開了我的身邊，可是沒有離開我的生活。你變成了我關門時的輕、走路時的慢、還有看到別人家毛孩時，那個忍不住的微笑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「永遠在心裡」這種容易空洞的話，換成具體可感的日常痕跡，比抽象金句更接得住
variations: 腳印留在地上會被雨沖掉，留在心上的不會。你那串腳印，我會帶著走一輩子。

en.content: You left my side, but you never left my life. You became the softness in how I close doors, the slowness in how I walk, and that smile I can't hold back whenever I see someone else's pet.
en.editorNote: Swaps the easily-hollow 'forever in my heart' line for concrete, felt daily traces — lands softer than an abstract aphorism
variations: Paw prints on the ground get washed away by rain. The ones on a heart don't. I'll carry yours for the rest of my life.

items.sourceNote: 「美好回憶大過悲傷」 frame — 牠從不帶來負面情緒，原創

zh-tw.content: 我試著回想你，發現難過的畫面只有最後那幾天，剩下的全是好笑的、溫暖的、傻乎乎的你。原來你連離開，都還在偷偷幫我把回憶調成溫的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 引導飼主從「卡在最後時刻」轉向「整段相處」，適合急性悲傷稍緩、開始能笑著回憶的階段
variations: 把相簿從頭滑到尾，我哭了，但更多是笑。你這輩子給我的好，多到悲傷分不到太多版面。

en.content: I tried to remember you and realized the sad scenes are only those last few days — everything else is the goofy, warm, ridiculous you. Turns out even in leaving, you quietly set my memories to 'warm.'
en.editorNote: Guides owners from being stuck in the final moments back to the whole relationship — for when acute grief eases and smiling-while-remembering becomes possible
variations: I scrolled the photo album end to end. I cried, but I laughed more. The good you gave me is so much that grief barely gets any screen time.

items.sourceNote: 「準備好那天再哭」 frame — 允許延後的悲傷，原創

zh-tw.content: 如果你今天還哭不出來，那也沒關係。悲傷不是限時搶購，它會等你準備好。你不欠任何人一場眼淚，包括你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給「為什麼我哭不出來、是不是不夠愛牠」的飼主，把延遲性悲傷 normalize，解除自我審判
variations: 有人當天就崩潰，有人是三個月後在超市看到罐頭才突然站不住。沒有正確的順序，只有你自己的節奏。

en.content: If you can't cry today, that's okay too. Grief isn't a flash sale — it'll wait until you're ready. You don't owe anyone tears, including yourself.
en.editorNote: For owners thinking 'why can't I cry, did I not love them enough' — normalizes delayed grief and lifts the self-judgment
variations: Some people break down that same day. Some lose their footing three months later, seeing a can of food at the grocery store. There's no correct order — only your own pace.

items.sourceNote: 「骨灰做成玻璃珠 / 項鍊」 frame — 把牠帶在身上的紀念品，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我把你的一小撮骨灰，做成了一顆掛在胸口的玻璃珠。不是要把你關起來，是想讓你離我的心跳近一點，近到我隨時低頭都看得見。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合「我做了一個紀念決定」的分享文，玻璃珠 / 骨灰項鍊 / 戒指都可套用，給猶豫的飼主一個溫柔的範例
variations: 不需要供在很遠的櫃子上。我選擇讓你變成一顆小小的、暖暖的、跟著我去任何地方的光。

en.content: I had a little of your ashes set into a glass bead I wear over my chest. Not to lock you away — just to keep you close to my heartbeat, close enough to see every time I look down.
en.editorNote: For 'I did a thing to remember them' posts; works for memorial beads, ash pendants, rings — gives a gentle example to owners on the fence
variations: I didn't want you on a shelf across the room. I chose to turn you into a small, warm bit of light that goes everywhere I go.

items.sourceNote: 「紀念角落 + 一盆植物」 frame — 不需要一次做完的紀念，原創

zh-tw.content: 我在窗邊幫你留了一個小角落：你的照片、一盆會慢慢長大的植物、還有那顆你以前咬到掉漆的球。我沒有急著一次做好。紀念你這件事，我想慢慢來，做一輩子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 回應「不知道怎麼開始紀念、怕做不夠好」的飼主，強調紀念可以是分層、漸進的，不是一個沉重的最終決定
variations: 不用一次就佈置得很完整。今天先放一張照片，哪天想了再加一盞燈、一朵花。這個角落會陪你一起慢慢長出來。

en.content: I made you a little corner by the window: your photo, a plant that'll slowly grow, and that ball you chewed the paint off of. I didn't rush to finish it. Remembering you isn't a deadline — it's something I want to do slowly, for the rest of my life.
en.editorNote: For owners frozen by 'I don't know how to start, what if it's not enough' — frames memorializing as layered and gradual, not one heavy final decision
variations: You don't have to build the whole shrine today. Set down one photo now, add a candle or a flower whenever you're ready. The corner grows alongside you.

items.sourceNote: 「帶骨灰回最愛的地方撒掉」 frame — 把牠帶回那條散步路，原創

zh-tw.content: 我帶你回到那條我們走過幾百次的河堤，把你輕輕還給了風。從今以後每次經過，我都會跟你說一聲嗨——你不在某個罐子裡了，你在整條路上等我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合決定撒骨灰 / 回最愛場景告別的飼主，把「失去骨灰」重新框成「牠回到了喜歡的地方」，化解不捨
variations: 不是把你撒掉，是把你放回你最開心的那片草地。以後那裡的風一吹，我就當作是你在跟我打招呼。

en.content: I took you back to that riverbank we walked a hundred times and gave you gently to the wind. Now every time I pass, I'll say hi — you're not in a jar anymore, you're waiting for me along the whole trail.
en.editorNote: For owners scattering ashes at a favorite spot; reframes 'losing the ashes' as 'returning them to where they were happiest' to ease the resistance
variations: I didn't scatter you away — I put you back in the field that made you happiest. Now when the wind moves there, I'll take it as you saying hello.

items.sourceNote: 「家人對紀念方式想法不同」 frame — 共同失去、不同的悲傷，原創

zh-tw.content: 我想把骨灰留在家，他想拿去海邊撒。我們差點為了這個吵起來，後來才懂——我們不是在吵怎麼紀念你，是兩個都太愛你、又都不知道怎麼放手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 處理「同住家人對後事 / 紀念方式有分歧」的場景，把衝突重新理解為共同的愛，鼓勵彼此體諒
variations: 一個家裡，可以有兩種思念的方式。你留一半在罐子裡、一半隨風走，沒有人輸，因為我們都還那麼想你。

en.content: I wanted to keep the ashes home; he wanted to scatter them at the sea. We almost fought over it — then realized we weren't arguing about how to remember you. We were just two people who loved you too much and didn't know how to let go.
en.editorNote: For households where family members disagree on memorial choices; reframes the conflict as shared love and encourages mutual grace
variations: One home can hold two kinds of missing. Keep half in the jar, give half to the wind — nobody loses, because we all still want you back this much.

items.sourceNote: 「領養日 / 點蠟燭的小儀式」 frame — 用固定的小動作維繫連結，原創

zh-tw.content: 每年到了把你接回家的那一天，我都會點一盞小蠟燭，放在你的照片旁邊，靜靜坐一下。不為了難過，是想對你說一聲：謝謝你那年願意跟我走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提供具體、低門檻、可長期重複的紀念儀式（領養紀念日點蠟燭），比抽象安慰更實用，適合衛教型貼文
variations: 不用很盛大。一盞燭光、一首你以前聽過的歌、坐五分鐘——這個小小的約定，會替我把你記得很久很久。

en.content: Every year on the day I brought you home, I light a small candle next to your photo and just sit for a while. Not to be sad — to say thank you for choosing to come with me back then.
en.editorNote: Offers a concrete, low-effort, repeatable ritual (a candle on adoption day) — more actionable than abstract comfort, good for mental-health-style posts
variations: It doesn't have to be grand. One candle, one song you used to hear, five minutes of sitting — this little standing date keeps you remembered for a long, long time.

items.sourceNote: A.A. Milne「幸好有讓告別這麼難的東西」frame，徹底改寫

zh-tw.content: 送你走的那天，我哭到站不穩。可是後來想想——會這麼捨不得，正是因為我曾經這麼幸運，擁有過一個讓告別這麼難的你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「告別很難」翻轉成「我很幸運」，適合喪寵後想寫一段話謝謝牠的飼主，正向但不假掰
variations: 如果告別不痛，那才代表我們之間什麼都沒發生過。會這麼痛，是這段關係給我的最後一份禮物。

en.content: The day I let you go, I cried so hard I couldn't stand. But later I understood — it only hurt this much because I was lucky enough to have something that made goodbye this hard.
en.editorNote: Flips 'goodbye is hard' into 'I was lucky' — for owners wanting to write a thank-you to their pet; positive without being saccharine
variations: If saying goodbye didn't hurt, it would mean nothing ever happened between us. The fact that it hurts this much is the last gift this bond gave me.

items.sourceNote: 「不是擁有，是租來的一段時間」frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 後來我才懂，我們從來沒有真正「擁有」一隻毛孩。我們只是借到了牠生命裡的一段時間——而牠把那段時間裡的全部，都給了我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「租來的時間」frame 幫飼主重新理解相處的本質，適合用在回望整段陪伴關係的長文
variations: 十幾年聽起來很長，回頭看卻短得像一場午睡。可是那場午睡裡，你從來沒有一秒不愛我。

en.content: I finally understood: we never really 'owned' them. We just borrowed a stretch of their life — and they spent every second of that stretch giving all of it to us.
en.editorNote: The 'borrowed time' frame reframes the whole relationship; good for a longer post looking back over the years together
variations: A decade sounds long, but looking back it was short as an afternoon nap. And in that nap, there wasn't one second you didn't love me.

items.sourceNote: 「失去的不只是牠，還有整套日常結構」secondary loss frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 我失去的不只是你，還有早上六點的散步、回家時門口的腳步聲、半夜踢到我腳邊那團溫溫的重量。原來一隻毛孩走了，會在生活裡留下這麼多個空格。
zh-tw.editorNote: 點出喪寵的「次級失落」——失去的是整套生活節奏，不只是牠本身。讓飼主知道自己的混亂是正常的
variations: 最難的不是想你的時候，是那些「本來該有你」卻突然空掉的時刻——餵食的鬧鐘、出門前的回頭、賴在沙發上的那個位置。

en.content: I didn't just lose you. I lost the 6 a.m. walk, the sound of paws at the door when I got home, the warm weight my foot bumped into at midnight. Turns out when a pet goes, they leave this many blanks in a day.
en.editorNote: Names the 'secondary loss' of pet grief — you lose the entire rhythm of your days, not just the animal. Reassures owners their disorientation is normal
variations: The hardest part isn't missing you. It's the moments that used to have you in them suddenly going empty — the feeding alarm, the glance back before leaving, that one spot on the couch.

items.sourceNote: 「每隻毛孩帶走一片心、也留下一片」frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 每一隻來過的毛孩，走的時候都帶走我心裡一小片；可是牠們進來的時候，也都留下了自己的一小片。所以這顆心其實沒有變空——它只是被好多份愛，重新拼過了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 改寫經典「狗帶走一片心」frame，回應「再養是不是背叛」的猶豫，給溫柔的許可
variations: 你帶走的那一片，我不要回來了。就讓它留在你那邊——這樣不管你在哪，都還有一小塊是我陪著你的。

en.content: Every pet who's passed through took a small piece of my heart when they left — but they each left a small piece of their own when they arrived. So my heart never actually emptied out. It just got pieced back together with a lot of love.
en.editorNote: Rewrites the classic 'every dog takes a piece of your heart' frame; answers the 'is adopting again a betrayal' hesitation with gentle permission
variations: The piece you took, I don't want back. Keep it over there — that way, wherever you are, there's still a little bit of me keeping you company.

items.sourceNote: anticipatory grief — 牠還在，但我已經開始想牠 frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 醫生說可能剩下幾個月。你還躺在我旁邊呼吸，我卻已經開始偷偷想你了。原來最難的不是失去，是明明還抱得到你，眼淚卻先掉下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給毛孩還在、但已進入倒數的飼主。預期性悲傷很少被談，這種「還在就先哭」的心情特別需要被看見
variations: 我開始把每一次散步都當成最後一次走。不是要嚇自己，是想確定——我沒有浪費掉任何一天還有你的日子。

en.content: The vet said maybe a few months left. You're still breathing right here next to me, and I've already started missing you in secret. Turns out the hardest part isn't losing you — it's crying while I can still hold you.
en.editorNote: For owners whose pet is still alive but on borrowed time. Anticipatory grief is rarely named — this 'crying while they're still here' feeling deserves to be seen
variations: I've started treating every walk like it might be the last one. Not to scare myself — to make sure I don't waste a single day I still get to have you.

items.sourceNote: 「把骨灰帶去牠最愛的地方」scattering frame，原創

zh-tw.content: 我帶著你去了那片你散步時每次都硬要往那衝的草地。風有點大，我把你輕輕撒出去，然後在原地站了很久。你終於可以想跑多遠就跑多遠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合「決定了骨灰怎麼安置」的分享文，把最私密的一步寫得溫柔、不沉重。地點可換成海邊、山上、家門口
variations: 我留了一小撮在項鍊裡，其他的還給了風。這樣你既可以自由地跑，也永遠掛在我胸口那個位置。

en.content: I took you back to that patch of grass you always yanked the leash toward. The wind was strong. I let you go gently, then just stood there a long while. You can finally run as far as you want.
en.editorNote: For 'we decided where the ashes would go' posts — makes the most private step feel tender rather than heavy. Location can be swapped for the beach, a trail, the front door
variations: I kept a little in a locket and gave the rest back to the wind. That way you get to run free and still hang right over my heart.

items.sourceNote: 「悲傷是愛在適應缺席」grief is love adjusting to absence frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 有人跟我說，悲傷不是一道要解開的題目。它只是愛，在學著適應你已經不在了。所以我不再急著「走出來」——我只是陪著這份愛，慢慢學會新的走路方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 回應「我是不是該好起來了」的焦慮，把悲傷重新定義成「愛在適應」，鬆開自我要求的壓力
variations: 我不需要「克服」你的離開。我只需要學會，怎麼帶著這份愛，繼續好好活下去。

en.content: Someone told me grief isn't a problem to be solved. It's just love, learning to adjust to your absence. So I stopped rushing to 'get over it' — I'm only keeping this love company while it learns a new way to walk.
en.editorNote: Answers the 'shouldn't I be better by now' anxiety by reframing grief as love adjusting; loosens the pressure to perform recovery
variations: I don't need to 'overcome' losing you. I just need to learn how to carry this love and keep living well anyway.

items.sourceNote: 「悲傷像海浪」grief comes in waves frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 悲傷像海浪。有時候海面很平，我以為自己好了；然後某個超市的罐頭轉角、某首歌、某個下雨天，浪就毫無預警地打過來，把我整個淹掉。沒關係，我學會了憋氣，也學會了等它退。
zh-tw.editorNote: 解釋喪寵悲傷的「非線性」——不是一路變好，而是忽好忽壞。讓飼主別因為突然崩潰而覺得自己退步了
variations: 別人以為過了三個月就該好了。可是悲傷沒有時刻表，它想什麼時候來，就什麼時候來。我不再罵自己「怎麼又哭了」。

en.content: Grief comes in waves. Sometimes the water's flat and I think I'm fine — then a corner of the pet-food aisle, a song, a rainy afternoon, and the wave crashes in with no warning and pulls me all the way under. It's okay. I've learned to hold my breath, and I've learned to wait for it to pull back out.
en.editorNote: Explains the non-linear nature of pet grief — not steady improvement but good days and ambush days. Stops owners from feeling they've 'relapsed' when a wave hits
variations: People assume three months in you should be fine. But grief doesn't run on a schedule — it shows up whenever it wants. I've stopped scolding myself for 'crying again.'

items.sourceNote: 「一部分靈魂被喚醒」Anatole France frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 遇見你以前，我心裡有一塊角落是暗的，自己都不知道。是你把那盞燈打開的。就算你走了，那盞燈也一直亮著，因為那是你留給我的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 改寫「愛過一隻動物，靈魂才被喚醒」的經典意象，適合當作長文追思或社群貼文開頭
variations: 你來之前，我不知道自己還能這樣愛一個生命。這件事你教會我了，這輩子都還給不完。

en.content: Before you, there was a dark little corner in me I didn't even know was there. You're the one who switched the light on. Even now that you're gone, that light stays on — because it's the one thing you left behind for me.
en.editorNote: Reworks the classic 'until you've loved an animal your soul stays unawakened' image; good as the opening of a longer tribute or social post
variations: Before you came, I didn't know I could love a life this much. You taught me that, and I'll never finish paying it back.

items.sourceNote: 寵物慰問卡「不知道說什麼」frame，原創改寫，安慰他人向

zh-tw.content: 我知道現在講什麼都補不回那個空位。所以我不勸你堅強，也不叫你別哭。我只是想讓你知道：你不用一個人扛，我隨時都在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給正在心碎的朋友的慰問卡或私訊，重點是「不急著給建議、只陪著」，避開「牠去更好的地方了」這種容易踩雷的話
variations: 我不會假裝懂那有多痛。但你需要有人聽你講講牠的時候，記得找我，我什麼時候都可以。

en.content: I know nothing I say can fill that empty spot. So I won't tell you to be strong, and I won't tell you not to cry. I just want you to know: you don't have to carry this alone. I'm right here, whenever.
en.editorNote: For a sympathy card or DM to a friend who's grieving; the point is to sit with them, not rush to advice, and to sidestep landmines like 'they're in a better place now'
variations: I won't pretend to know how much this hurts. But when you need someone to just listen to you talk about them, come find me. Any time at all.

items.sourceNote: 「不同房間」Paulo Coelho different rooms frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我一直覺得你沒有真的離開，只是走進了另一個房間。我暫時看不到你，但這棟房子還是我們一起住的。等哪天門開了，你會第一個跑出來迎接我，跟以前一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「死亡」改寫成「換到隔壁房間」，緩解永別的斷裂感，適合怕想到「再也見不到」而崩潰的飼主
variations: 你不是不見了，只是先進去另一個房間休息。愛沒有被牆隔開，它會一直穿過去找你。

en.content: I keep feeling like you never really left — you just walked into the next room. I can't see you for now, but it's still the house we live in together. And the day the door opens, you'll be the first one running out to greet me, exactly like before.
en.editorNote: Reframes death as 'moved to the room next door' to soften the finality; good for owners who spiral on 'I'll never see them again'
variations: You didn't vanish — you just stepped into the next room to rest. Love doesn't get stopped by a wall. It keeps passing through to find you.

items.sourceNote: 「有寵物就是先簽下悲傷」grief is the price of love frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 有人問我，早知道這麼痛，還會不會再養你一次。我想都不用想——會。這份痛，是我愛你的收據。要我用現在的心碎，換回從沒認識你，我一秒都不肯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 回應「養寵物到頭來只是換一場心碎」的自我懷疑，把痛重新定位成「值得的代價」，肯定飼主的選擇
variations: 現在的痛有多深，就代表當初的快樂有多滿。我不後悔，一次都不會後悔。

en.content: Someone asked me: if I'd known it would hurt this much, would I do it all over again. I didn't even have to think — yes. This pain is the receipt for how much I loved you. Trade this heartbreak to get back a life where I never met you? Not for one second.
en.editorNote: Answers the 'was getting a pet just signing up for heartbreak' self-doubt by reframing the pain as a price worth paying; affirms the owner's choice
variations: How deep it hurts now is just how full the happiness was back then. I don't regret it. Not once.

items.sourceNote: 「爪印停下、愛不會停」though the paws have stopped frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 你的小腳步聲停在走廊那頭了，可是我對你的愛沒有跟著停。它還在往前走，一天比一天更遠、更深。你把爪印留在地板上，也留在我心上，那個永遠洗不掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合刻紀念石、做腳印紀念品的配文，對比「腳步停了」與「愛不停」，收在溫柔的永恆感
variations: 家裡再也聽不到你的腳步聲，但你踩進我生命的那些印子，一輩子都在。

en.content: Your little footsteps went quiet at the far end of the hall — but my love for you didn't stop with them. It's still walking forward, a little farther and a little deeper every day. You left your pawprints on the floor, and on my heart, and that one never washes off.
en.editorNote: Good caption for a memorial stone or a pawprint keepsake; contrasts 'the steps stopped' with 'the love didn't,' landing on a gentle sense of forever
variations: I'll never hear your footsteps in the house again, but the prints you left walking into my life are here for good.

items.sourceNote: 「安慰朋友時別問要不要再養一隻」what-not-to-say frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 你不用急著好起來，也不用假裝沒事。牠對你有多重要，我都知道。我不會問你要不要再養一隻，只想坐在這裡陪你，你想哭就哭，想講牠的事我就聽，講多久都行。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給想安慰失去毛孩的朋友卻不知道怎麼開口的人；示範不催復原、不提替代、只給陪伴的說法
variations: 我不會叫你想開一點。牠值得你這樣難過。要陪要靜，你說一聲，我都在。

en.content: You don't have to bounce back, and you don't have to pretend you're fine. I know exactly how much they meant to you. I'm not going to ask if you'll get another one — I just want to sit here with you. Cry if you need to. Tell me about them for as long as you want; I've got time.
en.editorNote: For someone who wants to comfort a grieving pet owner but doesn't know what to say; models the no-rush, no-replacement, just-presence approach
variations: I won't tell you to look on the bright side. They're worth grieving this hard. Company or quiet — say the word, I'm here.

items.sourceNote: 「沒能好好說再見」的自責 reframe，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 你一直卡在「那天沒好好跟牠說再見」。可是牠從來不是靠那一句話認得你的愛。是每天摸頭、每頓飯、每次你回家牠衝過來——那些才是牠聽了一輩子的再見。你早就說過千萬遍了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 回應「走得太突然、沒能道別」的懊悔，把愛的證明從最後一刻挪回日常累積，鬆開飼主的自責
variations: 牠不是等你最後一句話才知道被愛的。牠早就知道了，每一天都知道。

en.content: You keep getting stuck on not saying a proper goodbye that day. But they never knew your love from one sentence. It was the head scratches, every meal, the way they charged the door when you came home — that was the goodbye they heard their whole life. You already said it a thousand times over.
en.editorNote: Answers the 'it happened too fast, I didn't get to say goodbye' regret by moving the proof of love off the last moment and onto the daily accumulation
variations: They didn't need your last words to know they were loved. They already knew — every single day.

items.sourceNote: 「還沒帶你去…、還沒拍全家福」未完成清單的遺憾 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我列了一張清單，全是「還沒帶你去的地方」：那片沒去成的海、講好要拍的全家福、明年再帶你回外婆家。清單很長，可是你陪我走過的每一天，一天也沒少。時間不夠，但愛從來沒有欠你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總想著「還來不及對牠更好」的飼主，承認遺憾但肯定已經給出的愛，避免把回憶只停在缺口上
variations: 我們是有好多事沒做完，可是我們一起做完的日子，也已經多到數不清了。

en.content: I made a list — all the places I never got to take you: the beach we missed, the family photo we kept meaning to shoot, the trip back to Grandma's next year. The list is long. But every single day you walked beside me, not one was missing. We ran out of time. We never ran out of love.
en.editorNote: For owners haunted by 'I never got to do enough for them'; acknowledges the regret while affirming the love already given, so memory doesn't stall on the gap
variations: Sure, there was a lot we never finished. But the days we did spend together — I lost count a long time ago.

items.sourceNote: 「幫忙處理實際瑣事」practical support offer frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 這幾天你不用一個人扛。晚餐我送過去，明天六點方便嗎？牠的碗和睡墊你先別急著收，等你準備好我陪你一起。你只要負責難過就好，剩下的雜事交給我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 示範「具體、可執行」的關心，取代空泛的『需要幫忙跟我說』；適合傳給剛送走毛孩的朋友
variations: 別客氣。跑腿、買東西、還是只是不想一個人待著，都算我一份。

en.content: You don't have to carry these next few days alone. I'll bring dinner over — does 6:00 tomorrow work? Don't rush to put away their bowl and bed; when you're ready, I'll do it with you. Your only job right now is to grieve. Leave the rest of the small stuff to me.
en.editorNote: Models specific, actionable care instead of a vague 'let me know if you need anything'; good to send a friend who just lost their pet
variations: Don't be shy about it. Errands, groceries, or just not wanting to be alone — count me in on all of it.

items.sourceNote: 「手還會習慣性伸向牽繩」日常慣性觸發思念 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 出門前，我的手還是會習慣性去拿牽繩。裝飯的時候，還是會先舀你那一份。這些小動作我停不下來——因為它們不是習慣，是我的身體記得怎麼愛你，而它到現在還沒忘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 捕捉喪寵後身體慣性帶來的細碎心痛，把「忘記牠已經走了」重新詮釋為愛留下的肌肉記憶，溫柔而不絕望
variations: 半夜還是會空出半張床給你。我知道你不在了，可是我的身體還在等你跳上來。

en.content: On my way out the door, my hand still reaches for the leash. Filling the food bowls, I still scoop your portion first. I can't stop these little motions — because they aren't habits. They're my body remembering how to love you, and it hasn't forgotten yet.
en.editorNote: Captures the small aches of post-loss muscle memory, reframing 'I keep forgetting they're gone' as love the body still carries; tender without tipping into despair
variations: I still leave half the bed open at night. I know you're gone, but my body's still waiting for you to jump up.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 你走了以後，家裡還是留著你的味道，我捨不得洗。原來想念一個人，也可以是想念一隻等門的身影。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合寵物離世初期、思念濃厚時的貼文或卡片
variations: 你不在了，我卻還是習慣進門先喊你的名字。
家裡少了你，安靜得讓人心疼。

en.content: You've been gone a while, but the house still smells like you, and I can't bring myself to wash it away. Turns out missing someone can mean missing a little shadow that always waited by the door.
en.editorNote: For the early, raw days of pet loss
variations: You're gone, and I still call your name every time I walk in.
The house is too quiet without you now.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 謝謝你陪我走過那幾年最難的日子，你什麼都不懂，卻懂我需要有人在。現在換我把你好好記著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合感謝毛孩陪伴、走出低潮後的紀念文
variations: 你不會說話，卻是最懂我的那一個。
有你在的那些年，我從來不算孤單。

en.content: Thank you for staying close through my hardest years. You didn't understand any of it, but you understood that I needed someone there. Now it's my turn to keep you close.
en.editorNote: For honoring a pet who got you through a rough patch
variations: You never said a word, and you still knew me best.
With you around, I was never really alone.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 我相信你只是先去了一個沒有病痛的地方，在那邊追你追不完的球、曬永遠溫暖的太陽。等哪天我也到了，記得第一個跑來找我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送別、彩虹橋主題的告別語
variations: 你先去那個不會痛的地方，我慢慢來，別急著等。
總有一天我們會再見，到時候換我陪你跑。

en.content: I like to think you just went ahead to a place with no more aches, chasing a ball that never stops rolling under a sun that's always warm. And when my day comes, be the first to run up and find me.
en.editorNote: For a rainbow-bridge style goodbye
variations: You went on ahead to somewhere that doesn't hurt. Take your time waiting for me.
One day we'll meet again, and I'll be the one chasing after you.

items.sourceNote: 獸醫「該慶幸是他先走」frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 還好是你先走。這樣所有的想念、所有的難過，都由我來扛就好。你不用去懂什麼是生離死別，不用找不到我、不明白我為什麼不見了。這些委屈，讓我一個人受就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放不下、覺得自己沒陪好的飼主，把「捨不得」轉成「保護他」
variations: 傷心的事我來就好，你只要記得被愛過就夠了。
換作是你留下來等我，那才叫殘忍。還好，先難過的人是我。

en.content: I'm glad you went first. This way all the missing, all the aching, it lands on me — and that's how it should be. You never had to learn what goodbye means, never had to wait by the door wondering why I stopped coming home. Let me carry that part alone.
en.editorNote: For the owner drowning in guilt; reframes 'I couldn't let go' into 'I'd rather it hurt me than you'
variations: Let the grief be mine. All you need to remember is that you were loved.
If you were the one left waiting for me — that would've been the cruel version. I'm glad I'm the one who has to miss you.

items.sourceNote: 「別對哀傷者說的話」frame（勿說再養一隻），原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 我不會叫你別哭，也不會說再養一隻就好。他不是一隻可以替換的寵物，他是你的家人。你想他多久都可以，我就坐在這裡陪你想。
zh-tw.editorNote: 寫給正在心碎的朋友，重點是不評價、不催他走出來
variations: 你不用堅強給我看，難過的時候有我在就好。
他佔的那個位置，本來就不是誰能補上的。你慢慢來。

en.content: I'm not going to tell you to stop crying, or that you can just get another one. He wasn't a pet you swap out — he was family. Miss him as long as you need. I'll sit right here and miss him with you.
en.editorNote: For a friend who just lost a pet; the whole point is no advice, no rushing them
variations: You don't have to be strong for me. Just let it hurt, I've got you.
The space he took up was never something anyone could fill. Take all the time you need.

items.sourceNote: 「空掉的日常」frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 最難的不是那一天，是隔天早上。手還是伸向那個空掉的角落，鑰匙轉開門卻沒有人衝過來。原來想念，是藏在每一個習慣裡的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合離世後一段時間、被日常細節突然擊中的飼主
variations: 我還是會多買一份、多留一個位子，然後才想起來。
碗收起來了，那個聲音還在。想你是沒有下班時間的。

en.content: The hardest part wasn't that day. It's the next morning — my hand still reaches for the empty corner, the key turns in the lock and nobody comes running. Turns out missing you is hiding inside every little habit.
en.editorNote: For an owner some weeks out, ambushed by ordinary moments
variations: I still buy one extra, still save a spot, and then I remember.
I put the bowl away, but the sound of you is still here. Missing you doesn't clock out.

items.sourceNote: 放下自責 frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 你不會怪我沒早點發現、不會怪我那天出門太久。在你眼裡我永遠是最好的那個人。所以拜託，也讓我試著相信一次——我已經做得夠好了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給困在「我是不是可以做更多」的飼主，替他說出毛孩的視角
variations: 你從來沒有一天覺得我不夠愛你，那我也不該。
如果連你都原諒我了，我是不是也可以放過自己。

en.content: You wouldn't blame me for not noticing sooner, or for being out too long that day. In your eyes I was always the best person in the world. So please — let me try, just once, to believe it too: I did enough.
en.editorNote: For the owner stuck on 'could I have done more'; voices the pet's forgiveness
variations: Not for a single day did you think I loved you too little. So maybe I shouldn't either.
If even you forgave me, maybe I'm allowed to let myself off the hook.

items.sourceNote: 「再愛一次不是背叛」frame，原創改寫

zh-tw.content: 有一天我可能會再牽起另一雙小手，那不是把你換掉，是你教會我的愛太滿了，滿到得再分一些出去。你永遠是第一個，這件事誰都取代不了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給糾結「再養一隻是不是對不起他」的飼主
variations: 再養一隻，不是忘了你，是想把你留給我的溫柔繼續傳下去。
第一格永遠寫著你的名字，後面的位子，是你讓我敢再填的。

en.content: One day I might hold another little paw. That's not replacing you — it's that the love you taught me got so full it had to spill over to someone new. You'll always be the first. Nothing and no one changes that.
en.editorNote: For the owner torn over whether adopting again betrays the one they lost
variations: Loving another one isn't forgetting you — it's passing on the softness you left in me.
The first slot has your name on it forever. You're the reason I'm brave enough to fill the rest.
